title: Search/filter debounce: 80ms requestIdleCallback
description: Filter recompute is O(library) (predicate over 10k); without debounce, each keystroke triggers a recompute. Add 80ms requestIdleCallback debounce; cancel pending recompute on each keystroke.
acceptanceCriteria: Typing in #search-input does not trigger filteredTracks() on every keystroke.
After 80ms of keyboard inactivity, recompute fires + Virtualizer.update() renders.
Subsequent keystrokes within 80ms cancel the pending callback (cancelIdleCallback).
Filter keystroke -> repaint < 50ms on 10k tracks (per PRD budget).
Vitest test asserts cancel-on-keystroke and final-render behavior.
Verified in browser using playwright: type 10 chars rapidly into search; only one filter recompute observed.

steps.details: _pendingFilterRecompute = null. Existing search input listener replaced with debounced version.

On input event: if (_pendingFilterRecompute) cancelIdleCallback(_pendingFilterRecompute); _pendingFilterRecompute = requestIdleCallback(() => { const indices = filteredTracks(); Virtualizer.update({totalCount: indices.length}); _pendingFilterRecompute = null; }, {timeout: 80}).

Rating filter, plays filter, last-played, My-Tag, phrase-only, beat-grid-only, audio-available - each one triggers the same debounced recompute. AppState.signal('filters') (existing) becomes the single entry point that the debouncer subscribes to.
tests/web/ui-logic.test.js: simulate 10 input events within 50ms; assert filteredTracks called only once after 80ms idle.

technicalNotes: requestIdleCallback gives the browser a hint to wait for idle time; timeout: 80 forces execution after 80ms regardless. Both bounds are important.
Don't use setTimeout - it competes with rendering; rIC defers to after paint.
